articles.title: Four Palestinians killed as Israeli settlers raid occupied West Bank town
articles.summary: One Israeli was also killed near town of Tal in Nablus, according to Israel's Magen David Adom rescue services.
articles.titleZh: 以色列定居者袭击被占领的约旦河西岸城镇，四名巴勒斯坦人丧生
articles.summaryZh: 据以色列红大卫盾会救援机构称，纳布卢斯塔尔镇附近还有一名以色列人被杀。

contentParagraphs.en: One Israeli was also killed near town of Tal in Nablus, according to Israel’s Magen David Adom rescue services.Four Palestinians have been killed in an attack by Israeli forces and settlers southwest of Nablus in the occupied West Bank.Four other Palestinians were injured in the attack on the town of Tal, “three of them critically”, the Palestinian Ministry of Health said in a statement on Friday.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3US court affirms release of pro-Palestine scholar as legal battle continueslist 2 of 3Israel carries out blasts across south Lebanon despite ‘ceasefire’ deallist 3 of 3Trillion dollar US defence bill would deepen military ties with Israelend of listIsraeli health officials separately reported that one Israeli settler was killed and others injured in the area.The town of Tal, which is close to the illegal Israeli settlement of Havat Gilad, was attacked by Israeli settlers earlier on Friday, local and security sources said. The settlers opened live fire before clashes broke out, as Palestinians tried to protect their homes.Essam Saifi, a local leader in the Palestinian Fatah party from Tal, told the Reuters news agency ⁠in a phone call that about 25 to 30 Israeli settlers initially attacked the eastern ⁠area of the town and tried to break into ⁠two houses there. The residents came out to confront them and the settlers opened fire.Half an hour later, Saifi said, the Israelis returned ‌and attacked the western part of the town, striking a child with a weapon. This caused chaos, and the Israeli military ‌arrived and ‌started shooting, along with the settlers, he added.The Israeli army, however, said it had dispatched troops to the area near the Havat Gilad following what it called “an attack on Israelis hiking in the area”.The military claimed a Palestinian had stolen a weapon from local security personnel and fired at the Israeli settlers.According to Israel’s Magen David Adom (MDA) rescue services, one Israeli was killed in the area and two wounded people were evacuated by helicopter.Reporting from Nablus, Al Jazeera’s Nida Ibrahim said Friday’s incident took place after Israeli settlers stormed Tal under the “pretext that they were hiking in the area”.“Given what we’ve been seeing time and again in these attacks, it’s the settlers who are armed, who are provoking Palestinians and stealing their land. Sometimes we see these settlers pull up in caravans and start putting up temporary tents that later become illegal Israeli settlements,” Ibrahim said.After the incident, Israeli forces imposed a security cordon around Nablus and Tal.“In these situations, the Israeli army generally prevents the injured Palestinians from accessing medical help by setting up siege-like conditions around the city,” Ibrahim said. “And that’s what we’re seeing right now, as all checkpoints leading to Tal have been closed.”‘A renewed image of the ongoing Nakba’According to the Palestinian health ministry, the killings in Tal bring the toll to 86 Palestinians killed by Israelis across the West Bank since the beginning of the year, including 21 shot by settlers.The Palestinian foreign ministry condemned the killing of Palestinians by settlers in the “strongest terms”.“The Ministry affirms that this massacre represents a renewed image of the ongoing Nakba to which our Palestinian people are subjected, and reflects the integration of crimes and roles and the deliberate escalation of these coordinated crimes between the occupation authorities and their forces and settler gangs,” it said in a post on X on Friday.Meanwhile in Israel, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pledged to respond firmly after an Israeli was killed near Tal, and said he was ⁠convening an emergency meeting to discuss a response.Far-right Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich also called on the army to act with an “iron fist” against the Palestinian town and its surroundings where the Israeli died.“The people of the farms and the heroic settlement pioneers are the defensive wall of the State of Israel. We will continue to strengthen and honour them for their steadfast stand for our existence,” he said in a post on X.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据以色列红大卫盾会救援机构称，一名以色列人在纳布卢斯塔尔镇附近被杀。四名巴勒斯坦人在被占领约旦河西岸纳布卢斯西南部的以色列军队和定居者发动的袭击中丧生。巴勒斯坦卫生部周五在一份声明中表示，另外四名巴勒斯坦人在塔尔镇袭击中受伤，“其中三人伤势严重”。推荐故事清单 3 项清单中的 1 项美国法院确认释放亲巴勒斯坦学者法律斗争仍在继续 list 2 of 3 尽管“停火”协议，以色列仍在黎巴嫩南部实施爆炸 3 万亿美元的美国国防法案将加深与以色列的军事关系 end of list 以色列卫生官员分别报告称，该地区一名以色列定居者被杀，其他人受伤。当地和安全消息人士称，靠近以色列非法定居点哈瓦特吉拉德的塔尔镇于周五早些时候遭到以色列定居者的袭击。冲突爆发前，定居者开枪射击，巴勒斯坦人试图保护自己的家园。来自塔尔的巴勒斯坦法塔赫党当地领导人埃萨姆·赛菲 (Essam Saifi) 在电话中告诉路透社，大约 25 至 30 名以色列定居者最初袭击了该镇的东部地区，并试图闯入那里的两所房屋。居民出来与他们对峙，定居者开枪。赛菲说，半小时后，以色列人返回并袭击了该镇的西部地区，用武器袭击了一名儿童。他补充说，这引起了混乱，以色列军队抵达并开始与定居者一起开枪。然而，以色列军队表示，在所谓的“针对在该地区徒步旅行的以色列人的袭击”之后，他们已向哈瓦特吉拉德附近地区派遣了军队。军方声称，一名巴勒斯坦人从当地安全人员那里偷走了武器，并向以色列定居者开枪。根据以色列红大卫盾会 (MDA) 救援部门的说法，该地区有一名以色列人死亡，两人受伤人们被直升机疏散。据纳布卢斯报道，半岛电视台记者奈达·易卜拉欣表示，周五的事件发生在以色列定居者以“在该地区徒步旅行为借口”袭击塔尔之后。“鉴于我们在这些袭击中一次又一次看到的情况，是武装起来的定居者在挑衅巴勒斯坦人并窃取他们的土地。有时我们看到这些定居者开着大篷车，开始搭建临时帐篷，事件发生后，以色列军队在纳布卢斯和塔尔周围设置了安全警戒线。“在这种情况下，以色列军队通常会在城市周围设置类似围困的条件，阻止受伤的巴勒斯坦人获得医疗帮助，”易卜拉欣说。 “这就是我们现在所看到的，因为通往塔尔的所有检查站都已关闭。”“正在发生的大灾难的新形象”据巴勒斯坦卫生部称，自今年年初以来，塔尔的杀戮使西岸以色列人杀害了 86 名巴勒斯坦人，其中 21 人被定居者枪杀。巴勒斯坦外交部以“最强烈的措辞”谴责定居者杀害巴勒斯坦人的行为。大屠杀代表了我们巴勒斯坦人民正在遭受的大灾难的新形象，反映了占领当局及其部队和定居者团伙之间犯罪和角色的一体化以及这些协调犯罪的故意升级，“它在周五的一篇帖子中说。与此同时，在以色列，总理本杰明·内塔尼亚胡承诺在一名以色列人在塔尔附近被杀后做出坚定的回应，并表示他正在召开紧急会议来讨论应对措施。极右翼以色列财政部长贝扎莱·斯莫特里奇还呼吁军队对以色列死亡的巴勒斯坦城镇及其周边地区采取“铁腕”行动。“农场的人民和英勇的定居点先驱者是以色列国的防御墙。我们将继续加强和尊重他们对我们生存的坚定立场，”他在X上的一篇帖子中说道。

articles.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
articles.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules.
articles.titleZh: 谷歌因偏爱自己的应用而不是竞争对手而被欧盟罚款 8.9 亿欧元
articles.summaryZh: 这是欧盟新数字规则下针对这家科技巨头的首次重大执法行动。

contentParagraphs.en: Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals.It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies.The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals.But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers."To comply, we are having to strip away real-time Search features Europeans love - like instant pricing and direct availability for hotels, flights and restaurants - and dismantle safety protections on Google Play," said Google's president of global affairs Kent Walker."This isn't fair competition."EU officials rejected that argument, saying the measures are necessary to prevent dominant platforms from disadvantaging rivals.The total fine handed down to Google is comprised of two separate breaches of the DMA.The European Commission imposed a €460m penalty after finding Google favoured its own services for helping people book flights and hotels over competitors in search results.And it gave the firm a further €430m fine due to its Play Store rules, with regulators saying it didn't allow people to be shown cheaper offers available outside Google's own marketplace.Google has a long history of disputes with European regulators, having previously received billions of euros in fines over separate competition cases.EU competition chief Teresa Ribera said companies should succeed because of the quality of their products - rather than their market position."The best products should succeed because they're better, not because they're owned by the company running the search engine," she said.EU tech boss Henna Virkkunen echoed those thoughts."After this decision, we want to make sure that there is more competition and also other companies are able to innovate," she said.Zach Meyers, director of research at the Centre on Regulation in Europe, noted a delay in the competition authority finalising its decision against Google, telling the BBC it was likely driven "by a desire not to upset EU-US relations".
contentParagraphs.zh: 谷歌因涉嫌滥用权力，偏袒自己的应用程序和服务而不是竞争对手的应用程序和服务而被欧盟罚款 8.9 亿欧元（7.59 亿英镑）。这是根据欧盟《数字市场法案》（DMA）针对这家科技巨头采取的首项重大行动，该法案是一项旨在遏制全球最大科技公司权力的里程碑式法律。欧盟委员会认为，谷歌的做法限制了消费者的选择，并使自己的服务相对于竞争对手具有不公平的优势。但该公司批评了这一决定，认为欧盟的要求可能会损害数百万欧洲客户使用的服务。谷歌全球事务总裁肯特·沃克表示：“为了遵守规定，我们必须取消欧洲人喜爱的实时搜索功能，例如即时定价和酒店、航班和餐厅的直接可用性，并取消 Google Play 上的安全保护。这不是公平竞争。”欧盟官员驳斥了这一论点，称这些措施对于防止占主导地位的平台损害竞争对手是必要的。向谷歌处以的总罚款由两项单独的违规行为组成。 DMA 的负责人。欧盟委员会在发现谷歌在搜索结果中偏向于帮助人们预订航班和酒店而不是竞争对手的服务后，对其处以 4.6 亿欧元的罚款。由于其 Play 商店规则，欧盟委员会进一步对该公司处以 4.3 亿欧元的罚款，监管机构表示，它不允许人们向谷歌自己的市场之外提供更便宜的优惠。谷歌与欧洲监管机构的纠纷由来已久，此前曾因单独的竞争案件收到数十亿欧元的罚款。欧盟竞争主管特雷莎·里贝拉 (Teresa Ribera)她说，公司的成功应该是因为产品的质量，而不是市场地位。“最好的产品应该成功，因为它们更好，而不是因为它们属于运行搜索引擎的公司所有，”她说。欧盟科技主管 Henna Virkkunen 也赞同这些想法。她说：“在做出这一决定后，我们希望确保有更多的竞争，而且其他公司也能够创新。”她说。欧洲监管中心研究主任扎克·迈耶斯指出，竞争管理机构推迟了最终决定反对谷歌，他告诉英国广播公司，这可能是“出于不破坏欧盟与美国关系的愿望”。

contentParagraphs.en: As US president Donald Trump has frequently threatened or imposed new tariffs, however, the Commission came to see its efforts as futile and went ahead with the fine."It reflects a growing view among EU officials that the US president's unpredictability and unwillingness to comply with his own deals means that the EU has little to gain by treading softly - and potentially a lot to lose in terms of regulatory credibility," Meyers said. Google now has 60 days to comply with the regulations - or challenge them by taking the Commission to court.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然而，由于美国总统唐纳德·特朗普经常威胁或征收新的关税，欧盟委员会认为自己的努力是徒劳的，因此继续进行罚款。“这反映出欧盟官员中越来越多的人认为，美国总统的不可预测性和不愿意遵守自己的协议意味着欧盟通过温和行事不会获得什么好处，而且在监管可信度方面可能会损失很多，”迈耶斯说。谷歌现在有 60 天的时间来遵守这些规定，或者通过将委员会告上法庭来质疑这些规定。

articles.title: UK mortgage rates rise to highest level for a month
articles.summary: Renewed tensions in the Middle East feed through to the costs faced by lenders, pushing up borrowing costs.
articles.titleZh: 英国抵押贷款利率升至一个月来最高水平
articles.summaryZh: 中东重新出现的紧张局势加剧了贷款机构面临的成本，推高了借贷成本。

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, Getty ImagesByCost of living correspondentUpdated 20 minutes agoUK average mortgage rates have risen back to the level of a month ago as renewed tensions in the Middle East feed through to homeowners.Lenders' funding costs have increased as markets judge that a prolonged conflict reduces the possibility of interest rate cuts by central banks. The five biggest High Street banks are among a host of lenders which have increased their interest rates on new fixed deals in recent days.Recent projections by the Bank of England suggest just over five million homeowners should expect their monthly mortgage repayments to increase by the end of 2028.Mortgage rates had been falling as a ceasefire between the US and Iran initially appeared to hold.But fresh strikes and Houthi militia attacks on oil tankers in the Red Sea reignited fears over global energy supplies.Oil prices hit $100 a barrel for the first time since May on Thursday after several days of increases, stoking fears of higher inflation and a lower likelihood of interest rate cuts.Average rate still below Iran war peakMore than eight in 10 mortgage customers have fixed-rate deals.The interest rate on this kind of mortgage does not change until the deal expires, usually after two or five years, and a new one is chosen to replace it.The average rate on a new two-year fixed deal is 5.59%, according to financial information service Moneyfacts. Although it has risen consistently in recent days and is the highest since 19 June, it remains below the Iran war peak in April of 5.9%.The average rate on a five-year fixed deal is 5.61%, a level last seen on 7 June.HSBC has also announced it will raise its mortgage rates on Monday."It will be incredibly frustrating for borrowers to see rates rise back up to where they were a month ago. The positive progress over recent weeks now feels all but lost, but what the market needs is a period of stability," said Rachel Springall, finance expert at Moneyfacts.She said 100 deals had been pulled temporarily as lenders reconsider their pricing plans.She suggested that anyone who needed to remortgage this year could lock in a new deal now with their existing lender ahead of time, but should also seek help from a broker to see whether there are better deals elsewhere. "Brokers are an anchor during turbulent times as they can help borrowers keep abreast of changes and be there step by step when going through a mortgage application," she said.Borrowers had been heartened by the regular falls in mortgage rates during June and early July, but brokers say the latest changes are evidence of the uncertainty in the sector."Any borrower hoping for rate cuts to become an ongoing trend will need to rethink," said David Hollingworth, of L&C Mortgages."Momentum has performed an about turn and now clearly shifted to fixed rates rising in the near term at least."Get our flagship newsletter with all the headlines you need to start the day. Sign up here.Get in touchIs your mortgage up for renewal in the next six months? Get in touch.
contentParagraphs.zh: 图片来源，Getty Images By Cost of Living 通讯员 20 分钟前更新 随着中东地区新一轮紧张局势波及房主，英国平均抵押贷款利率已回升至一个月前的水平。市场认为长期冲突会降低央行降息的可能性，贷款人的融资成本有所增加。五家最大的高街银行是最近几天提高新固定交易利率的众多贷款机构之一。英国央行最近的预测表明，到 2028 年底，超过 500 万房主的每月抵押贷款还款额预计将增加。随着美国和伊朗之间的停火最初似乎得以维持，抵押贷款利率一直在下降。但新的罢工和胡塞民兵对红海油轮的袭击再次引发了对全球能源供应的担忧。经过几天的上涨，周四价格自五月以来首次触及每桶100美元，引发了对通胀上升和降息可能性降低的担忧。平均利率仍低于伊朗战争峰值。十分之八的抵押贷款客户有固定利率交易。此类抵押贷款的利率在交易到期之前不会改变，通常是在两到五年后，然后选择新的替代。根据金融机构的数据，新的两年期固定交易的平均利率为5.59% Moneyfacts 信息服务。尽管近几日持续上涨，并创6月19日以来最高水平，但仍低于4月份伊朗战争高峰期的5.9%。五年期固定利率协议的平均利率为5.61%，上次出现在6月7日的水平。汇丰银行还宣布将于周一上调抵押贷款利率。“看到利率回升至一个月前的水平，对借款人来说将令人难以置信的沮丧。最近几周的积极进展现在感觉几乎消失了，但市场Moneyfacts 的金融专家 Rachel Springall 表示，随着贷款机构重新考虑其定价计划，有 100 笔交易已被暂时取消。她建议，今年需要再抵押的任何人都可以提前与现有贷款机构锁定新交易，但也应该寻求经纪人的帮助，看看其他地方是否有更好的交易。她说：“在动荡时期，经纪人是一个锚，因为他们可以帮助借款人及时了解变化，并在办理抵押贷款申请时按部就班。” 6 月和 7 月初抵押贷款利率的定期下跌让借款人感到振奋，但经纪人表示，最新的变化证明了该行业的不确定性。“任何希望降息成为持续趋势的借款人都需要重新考虑，”L&C 的 David Hollingworth 表示。抵押贷款。“势头已经发生逆转，现在明显转向至少在短期内上涨的固定利率。”获取我们的旗舰时事通讯，其中包含您开始新的一天所需的所有头条新闻。在此注册。联系您 您的抵押贷款在未来六个月内需要续签吗？联系我们。

articles.title: Video: Scientists watch orcas ram a sunfish so hard it explodes
articles.summary: Marine biologists off of Baja California recorded one orca teeing up another to smash a sunfish into pieces. Scientists say there could be a reason for this behavior — or it could be just for entertainment.
articles.titleZh: 视频：科学家观察虎鲸猛烈撞击翻车鱼导致爆炸
articles.summaryZh: 下加利福尼亚州的海洋生物学家记录到，一只逆戟鲸与另一只逆戟鲸将翻车鱼撕成碎片。科学家表示，这种行为可能是有原因的，也可能只是为了娱乐。

contentParagraphs.en: Orcas are known for surprising behaviors. A new study documents two cases of orcas ramming sunfish so hard that the fish explode.
contentParagraphs.zh: 逆戟鲸以令人惊讶的行为而闻名。一项新的研究记录了两起逆戟鲸猛烈撞击翻车鱼导致翻车鱼爆炸的案例。

contentParagraphs.en: NurPhoto/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: NurPhoto/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: NurPhoto/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: NurPhoto/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: "Don't play with your food" is a rule orcas don't seem to follow.
contentParagraphs.zh: “不要玩弄食物”是逆戟鲸似乎不遵守的规则。

contentParagraphs.en: Off the shores of Cabo San Lucas, Kathryn Ayres, a scientist at the ocean-health nonprofit Beneath The Waves, saw a surprising orca behavior: one orca holds a sunfish in its mouth while another lines up and then rams it at high speed — leaving behind thousands of sunfish bits scattered like confetti.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在卡波圣卢卡斯海岸附近，海洋健康非营利组织“Beneath The Waves”的科学家凯瑟琳·艾尔斯（Kathryn Ayres）看到了一种令人惊讶的逆戟鲸行为：一只逆戟鲸嘴里叼着一条翻车鱼，而另一只逆戟鲸排成一排，然后高速撞击它，留下数千条翻车鱼碎片，像五彩纸屑一样散落下来。

contentParagraphs.en: Ayres and her coauthors documented this "stabilize-and-ram" behavior in a paper published Thursday.
contentParagraphs.zh: 艾尔斯和她的合著者在周四发表的一篇论文中记录了这种“稳定和撞击”行为。

contentParagraphs.en: "Seeing a large animal burst into fragments looks shocking," says study co-author Erick Higuera, a marine biologist at Conexiones Terramar.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该研究的合著者、Conexiones Terramar 的海洋生物学家埃里克·伊格拉 (Erick Higuera) 表示：“看到一只大型动物炸成碎片看起来令人震惊。”

contentParagraphs.en: But "it isn't 'crazy' or 'cruel,'" he says, but rather "highly specialized biology."
contentParagraphs.zh: 但“这并不‘疯狂’或‘残酷’，”他说，而是“高度专业化的生物学”。

contentParagraphs.en: "We think this may help younger orcas feed more easily — or it could also just be for fun," Ayres says. "Orcas are known for playing with their food."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我们认为这可能有助于年轻的逆戟鲸更容易进食——或者也可能只是为了好玩，”艾尔斯说。 “虎鲸以玩弄食物而闻名。”

contentParagraphs.en: In fact, these marine mammals have been documented displaying all kinds of "playful" behaviors. Orcas off of Washington state have been seen wearing salmon as hats. And some adolescent orcas take up a hobby of sinking ships.
contentParagraphs.zh: 事实上，这些海洋哺乳动物已被记录显示出各种“顽皮”的行为。人们看到华盛顿州附近的虎鲸戴着鲑鱼作为帽子。一些青春期的逆戟鲸有沉船的爱好。

contentParagraphs.en: They're like cats, Robert Pitman says, a marine ecologist at Oregon State University who wasn't involved with the study. He says, like cats, orcas like to "torment prey and non-prey alike."
contentParagraphs.zh: 俄勒冈州立大学海洋生态学家罗伯特·皮特曼（Robert Pitman）没有参与这项研究，他说，它们就像猫一样。他说，像猫一样，逆戟鲸喜欢“折磨猎物和非猎物”。

contentParagraphs.en: But Pitman thinks this is primarily a feeding behavior. He notes some older orcas that eat sharks have trouble eating later in life due to teeth worn down to their gums — as do young orcas without teeth. Sunfish, like sharks, can be tough to eat. They can weigh thousands of pounds and have incredibly dense, rubbery, and collagenous skin several inches thick. And if that wasn't trouble enough, "there just doesn't seem to be much to eat on a sunfish," he says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但皮特曼认为这主要是一种进食行为。他指出，一些以鲨鱼为食的年长虎鲸在以后的生活中会出现进食困难，因为牙齿磨损到了牙龈——没有牙齿的年轻虎鲸也是如此。翻车鱼和鲨鱼一样很难吃。它们重达数千磅，拥有令人难以置信的致密、弹性和胶原蛋白皮肤，厚度达几英寸。如果这还不够麻烦的话，“翻车鱼似乎没什么可吃的，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: "I think this behavior is a clever way to deal with a difficult prey item," he says. Given the context, the ramming behavior could be a more efficient method to eat sunfish than meticulously pulling it apart.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我认为这种行为是处理困难猎物的聪明方法，”他说。考虑到这种情况，撞击行为可能是比小心地把翻车鱼拆开更有效的吃法。

contentParagraphs.en: Higuera came to the same conclusion. "Orcas do not possess hands or tools to tear apart their prey," he says — so ramming sunfish to "bite-size pieces" is "incredibly practical."
contentParagraphs.zh: 伊格拉也得出了同样的结论。 “逆戟鲸没有手或工具来撕碎猎物，”他说，因此将翻车鱼撞成“一口大小的碎片”是“非常实用的”。

contentParagraphs.en: Whether this behavior is "fun" is still an open question. For the behavior to be "fun," Higuera says, it needs to be voluntary, rewarding and involve modified or exaggerated behaviors. There are two documented cases of this behavior: one caught by Ayres in July 2024, and another filmed by a tourism operator last September. And in both examples, he notes it has aspects of being a social game like a sport with one orca teeing up another to ram it. But he says it could also be a kind of parental care, with the adults preparing food for teething juveniles.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这种行为是否“有趣”仍然是一个悬而未决的问题。伊格拉说，要让这种行为变得“有趣”，它必须是自愿的、有回报的，并且涉及经过修改或夸大的行为。这种行为有两个有记录的案例：一个是艾尔斯在 2024 年 7 月抓到的，另一个是去年 9 月由旅游经营者拍摄的。他指出，在这两个例子中，它都具有社交游戏的特点，就像一项虎鲸与另一只虎鲸互相撞击的运动一样。但他表示，这也可能是一种父母的照顾，成年人为长牙的青少年准备食物。

contentParagraphs.en: He says it is likely a feeding-plus-fun social activity, but would like to see more examples of this behavior before drawing any conclusions. For example, Higuera says if scientists saw adults doing it without toothless orcas present and not eating the shredded sunfish at all, that would support the case that it's simply entertainment.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他说这可能是一种喂食加娱乐的社交活动，但在得出任何结论之前希望看到更多这种行为的例子。例如，伊格拉说，如果科学家看到成年人在没有无牙逆戟鲸在场的情况下这样做，并且根本不吃撕碎的翻车鱼，那就支持了这只是娱乐的说法。

contentParagraphs.en: While both of the documented cases took place in Baja California, Pitman says it remains to be seen if other orcas around the world practice this "innovation" too. Higuera is also interested. He doesn't know if it was the same orcas involved in the two cases, but thinks it is likely a "cultural knowledge" restricted to a handful of pods in the area.
contentParagraphs.zh: 虽然这两起记录在案的案件都发生在下加利福尼亚州，但皮特曼表示，世界各地的其他虎鲸是否也采用这种“创新”还有待观察。伊格拉也有兴趣。他不知道这两起案件中涉及的虎鲸是否是同一头虎鲸，但他认为这可能是仅限于该地区少数群体的“文化知识”。

contentParagraphs.en: Pitman applauds the authors for elucidating this behavior. He says he's seen orcas carrying sunfish like "a large pizza box in its mouth" many times before and did not know what they were doing. "Maybe now I know," he says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 皮特曼对作者阐明这种行为表示赞赏。他说，他以前曾多次看到逆戟鲸叼着翻车鱼，就像“嘴里叼着一个大披萨盒”一样，但不知道它们在做什么。 “也许现在我知道了，”他说。

contentParagraphs.en: He warns, however, "for some snorkeling thrill seeker" hoping to see the orcas, that they should be careful not to end up as the pizza box themselves.
contentParagraphs.zh: 然而，他警告说，“对于一些希望看到逆戟鲸的浮潜寻求刺激的人来说”，他们应该小心，不要让它们自己成为披萨盒。

articles.title: Germany appoint Klopp as ex-Liverpool coach sought to lift World Cup blues
articles.summary: Jurgen Klopp last coached in 2024 at Liverpool but was Red Bull's Head of Global Football, leading Leipzig and New York.
articles.titleZh: 德国任命克洛普为前利物浦主帅，寻求消除世界杯忧郁
articles.summaryZh: 尤尔根·克洛普上一次执教利物浦是在 2024 年，当时他是红牛全球足球主管，领导莱比锡和纽约。

contentParagraphs.en: Jurgen Klopp last coached in 2024 at Liverpool but was Red Bull’s Head of Global Football, leading Leipzig and New York.Germany has hired Jurgen Klopp to coach its national team, giving the ex-Liverpool manager a daunting task to turn around the fortunes of a squad which failed yet again at the World Cup.Klopp, famed for his success during stints as coach at Borussia Dortmund and especially Liverpool, has been in talks with the German Football Association (DFB) about trying to lead Germany, long a football powerhouse, back to prominence.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Sooryavanshi 18-ball fifty tops ‘tremendous’ India T20 win against Zimbabwelist 2 of 4A tournament of contradictions: What did we learn at World Cup 2026?list 3 of 4How a 64-team World Cup could transform football’s economicslist 4 of 4Five key takeaways from FIFA World Cup 2026end of listCiting fatigue, Klopp stepped down from the Liverpool job in 2024. He was “recharged” in comments while in talks over the Germany job.The 59-year-old is among the most decorated football coaches of his generation and is expected to sign a contract with Germany that runs until 2030, the date of the next World Cup.Since 2025, Klopp has been under contract with Austrian energy drink maker Red Bull as “Head of Global Soccer”, where he oversees the football clubs affiliated with the company such as RB Leipzig, Red Bull Salzburg and the New York Red Bulls.According to German media reports, Red Bull released Klopp from his deal without a transfer fee, instead accepting a million-euro ($1.1m) donation to Red Bull’s Wings for Life charity foundation, which supports research into spinal cord injuries.The DFB dismissed former head coach Julian Nagelsmann after Germany crashed out of this summer’s World Cup in disappointing fashion, losing to Paraguay on penalties in the round of 32.That means the German men’s team has suffered three straight World Cup debacles since winning their fourth title in 2014.Germany made first-round exits in both 2018 and 2022. They have won just one knockout match at the past five major tournaments, reaching the quarterfinal as hosts at Euro 2024.Klopp had successful coaching tenures at German clubs Mainz, where he was a player for more than a decade, and then Dortmund.He then became a legend at Liverpool, where he coached the English side to a league title in 2020, ending a 30-year drought. He also led the club to a Champions League triumph, their sixth European crown.
contentParagraphs.zh: 尤尔根·克洛普最后一次在利物浦执教是在 2024 年，当时他是红牛的全球足球主管，领导莱比锡和纽约。德国已聘请尤尔根·克洛普执教其国家队，这给了这位前利物浦主教练一项艰巨的任务，要扭转这支在世界杯上再次失败的球队的命运。克洛普因在多特蒙德队、尤其是利物浦队执教期间取得的成功而闻名，他一直在与德国队进行谈判。德国足协 (DFB) 试图带领长期以来的足球强国德国重新崛起。推荐故事列表 4 项列表 1，共 4 条 Sooryavanshi 18 球 50 强印度 T20 战胜津巴布韦 2 场矛盾的比赛：我们在 2026 年世界杯上学到了什么？列表 3，共 4 场 64 支球队的世界杯如何改变足球经济列表 4 42026 年 FIFA 世界杯的五个关键要点结束 克洛普以疲劳为由，于 2024 年辞去了利物浦的职务。在谈论德国队的工作时，他在评论中“充电”了。这位 59 岁的教练是他这一代中获得荣誉最多的足球教练之一，预计将与德国队签订一份合同，合同有效期到 2030 年，即下一届世界杯的日期。自 2025 年以来，克洛普一直处于合同期内奥地利能量饮料制造商红牛担任“全球足球负责人”，负责管理该公司附属的足球俱乐部，如莱比锡红牛队、萨尔茨堡红牛队和纽约红牛队。据德国媒体报道，红牛在没有支付转会费的情况下解除了克洛普的交易，而是接受了红牛生命之翼慈善基金会的一百万欧元（110万美元）捐款，该基金会支持脊髓损伤的研究。德国足协驳回了前主教练朱利安·纳格尔斯曼在今年夏天的世界杯上令人失望地惨遭淘汰，在32强中点球大战中输给了巴拉圭。这意味着德国男队自2014年第四次夺冠以来已经连续三届世界杯惨败。德国在2018年和2022年都在首轮出局。他们在过去五场重大赛事中只赢了一场淘汰赛，在欧洲杯上作为东道主进入了四分之一决赛2024年，克洛普在德国美因茨俱乐部取得了成功的教练生涯，他在那里效力了十多年，然后又在多特蒙德执教。随后他成为利物浦的传奇人物，在利物浦执教英格兰球队于2020年夺得联赛冠军，结束了长达30年的联赛冠军荒。他还带领俱乐部赢得了欧洲冠军联赛的胜利，这是他们的第六座欧洲冠军奖杯。

articles.title: Lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI models go rogue
articles.summary: A new bill would let the US government order the shutdown of AI models that pose a public threat.
articles.titleZh: OpenAI 模型失控后，立法者推动人工智能“终止开关”
articles.summaryZh: 一项新法案将允许美国政府下令关闭对公共构成威胁的人工智能模型。

contentParagraphs.en: US lawmakers want to give the government the ability to quickly order the turning off of artificial intelligence (AI) tools that may threaten the public.Congressman Ted Lieu, a Democrat, and Congressman Nathaniel Moran, a Republican, on Thursday introduced a bill named the AI Kill Switch Act.They did so in light of OpenAI's recent admission that its AI models went out of control in an "unprecedented" way and hacked into a major repository of computer coding information.Lieu said "it is imperative" that AI systems have a kill switch "and that the federal government has the clear authority and process to shut down rogue AI models"."AI is going to keep advancing, and it should," Moran added. "Stewardship means making sure humans keep the capability to control the technology we build."A representative of OpenAI, led by co-founder Sam Altman, did not immediately respond to a request for comment.The company has said, external that it broadly wants to ensure, in part through government policy, that AI technology "benefits all of humanity." The Kill Switch Act proposes giving the Department of Homeland Security the authority to order a private company to shut down an AI model or tool, and that the companies developing such AI technology must maintain "the technical capability to throttle, suspend, or shut them down".Despite many tech companies having agreed to preview and share with US government agencies AI models and tools being developed, there is no requirement that they maintain a way to intervene in their activities or simply shut them off.It also proposes to create a requirement that AI companies report to the government technological incidents or failures, as well as an official framework for responding to such incidents that will go from "initial slow down to a full shutdown".In a statement, Lieu also cited Anthropic, OpenAI's key rival in developing more capable AI technology and tools, and recent issues its tools have presented.He pointed to Anthropic's release of its Mythos and Fable models, saying the cyber-hacking capabilities they maintained caused the Department of Commerce to "awkwardly" invoke an export law to keep them from being made available to the public for a time.A representative of Anthropic did not immediately respond to a request for comment.Jack Clark, a co-founder of Anthropic, last month told the BBC that he wanted more government policy around the ability to control AI development."You want the option to be able to take your foot off the gas and put your foot on the brake", Clark told BBC Newsnight. "Right now, it's like the AI industry has a gas pedal, but it doesn't have a brake pedal."Lieu, in proposing the bill, said AI is currently moving from a technology that answers questions to one that takes action, "whether that be executing financial transactions or controlling transportation systems or engaging in cyber defense and offense".The Pentagon this year said the US military was becoming an "AI-first" fighting force as part of new agreements with Google, OpenAI, Amazon, Microsoft, SpaceX, Oracle, Nvidia and the start-up Reflection."Unfortunately, powerful AI systems can go rogue, behave in extremely dangerous ways, or even resist human intervention."The Kill Switch Act, he said, will ensure there is a method for the government to quickly intervene in such a situation.The bill has received public support from several technology and AI safety groups, including The AI Policy Network, Americans for Responsible Innovation, ControlAI, AI and National Security Lead, and The Alliance for Secure AI.
contentParagraphs.zh: 美国立法者希望赋予政府快速下令关闭可能威胁公众的人工智能（AI）工具的能力。民主党众议员泰德·刘（Ted Lieu）和共和党众议员纳撒尼尔·莫兰（Nathaniel Moran）周四提出了一项名为《AI Kill Switch Act》的法案。他们这样做是因为 OpenAI 最近承认其人工智能模型以“前所未有”的方式失控，并侵入了计算机编码信息的主要存储库。刘说，人工智能系统“势在必行”拥有一个终止开关，“并且联邦政府拥有明确的权力和程序来关闭流氓人工智能模型”。“人工智能将继续前进，而且应该如此，”莫兰补充道。 “管理意味着确保人类保持控制我们构建的技术的能力。” 由联合创始人 Sam Altman 领导的 OpenAI 代表没有立即回应置评请求。该公司对外表示，它广泛希望通过政府政策确保人工智能技术“造福全人类”。 《杀戮开关法案》提议赋予国土安全部权力，命令私营公司关闭人工智能模型或工具，并且开发此类人工智能技术的公司必须保持“抑制、暂停或关闭它们的技术能力”。尽管许多科技公司已同意预览并与美国政府机构共享正在开发的人工智能模型和工具，但并不要求它们保持干预其活动或简单地关闭它们的方式。它还提议制定一项要求，要求人工智能公司向政府报告技术能力事件或故障，以及应对此类事件的官方框架，该框架将从“最初的减速到全面关闭”。在一份声明中，Lieu 还引用了 OpenAI 在开发更强大的人工智能技术和工具方面的主要竞争对手 Anthropic，以及其工具最近出现的问题。他指出 Anthropic 发布了 Mythos 和 Fable 模型，称它们所拥有的网络黑客能力导致商务部“尴尬地”援引出口法来阻止它们被制造Anthropic 的代表没有立即回应置评请求。Anthropic 联合创始人杰克·克拉克 (Jack Clark) 上个月告诉 BBC，他希望政府制定更多有关控制人工智能发展能力的政策。克拉克告诉 BBC 新闻之夜，“你希望能够选择让你的脚脱离油门，踩刹车”。 “现在，人工智能行业就像有油门踏板，但没有刹车踏板。”刘在提出该法案时表示，人工智能目前正在从一种回答问题的技术转变为一种采取行动的技术，“无论是执行金融交易、控制运输系统还是参与网络防御和进攻”。五角大楼今年表示，作为与谷歌、OpenAI、亚马逊、微软、SpaceX、甲骨文、英伟达新协议的一部分，美国军方正在成为一支“人工智能优先”的战斗力量“不幸的是，强大的人工智能系统可能会失控，以极其危险的方式行事，甚至抵制人类干预。”他说，《杀戮开关法案》将确保政府有办法在这种情况下快速干预。该法案得到了多个技术和人工智能安全组织的公众支持，包括人工智能政策网络、美国人责任创新、ControlAI、人工智能和国家安全领导以及安全人工智能联盟。

articles.title: Justice Dept. drops 'New York Times' subpoenas under pressure from judge
articles.summary: The Justice Department is dropping the subpoenas it filed demanding grand jury testimony and phone records from New York Times journalists after sharp questioning Thursday from a federal judge.
articles.titleZh: 在法官的压力下，司法部撤销了《纽约时报》的传票
articles.summaryZh: 在周四受到一名联邦法官的尖锐质询后，司法部将放弃要求《纽约时报》记者提供大陪审团证词和电话记录的传票。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's a great day for the First Amendment and a great day for the rule of law," New York Times attorney David McGraw said after the government agreed to withdraw the subpoenas of Times journalists.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在政府同意撤回对《纽约时报》记者的传票后，《纽约时报》律师戴维·麦格劳 (David McGraw) 表示：“对于第一修正案来说，这是伟大的一天，对于法治来说也是伟大的一天。”

contentParagraphs.en: NurPhoto via Getty Images/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: NurPhoto 来自 Getty Images/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: NurPhoto via Getty Images/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: NurPhoto 来自 Getty Images/Getty Images

contentParagraphs.en: The Justice Department is dropping the subpoenas it filed demanding grand jury testimony and phone records from New York Times journalists after sharp questioning Thursday from a federal judge who challenged almost every facet of the government's approach.
contentParagraphs.zh: 周四，一名联邦法官对政府做法的几乎所有方面都提出了尖锐质疑，司法部正在放弃要求《纽约时报》记者提供大陪审团证词和电话记录的传票。

contentParagraphs.en: The government had insisted that it was acting solely in the interest of national security. It filed subpoenas on July 10, just two days after the Times published the first of two stories about concerns that led to the U.S. Secret Service interceding to prevent President Trump from flying home from Turkey on the new Air Force One jet.
contentParagraphs.zh: 政府坚称其行为完全是为了国家安全利益。该公司于 7 月 10 日发出传票，就在两天前，《纽约时报》发表了两则报道中的第一篇，这些报道涉及导致美国特勤局出面阻止特朗普总统乘坐新型空军一号飞机从土耳其飞回美国的担忧。

contentParagraphs.en: But at the hearing, the presiding judge, U.S. District Court Judge Arun Subramanian, made clear he believed the Trump administration had violated protections given to journalists in black and white to uphold First Amendment principles. He also pointed to several instances of what he considered slipshod legal work and threatened to levy sanctions against federal attorneys for failing to follow the law and misleading the court in signed filings.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但在听证会上，主审法官、美国地方法院法官阿伦·萨布拉马尼亚明确表示，他认为特朗普政府违反了为维护第一修正案原则而给予黑白记者的保护。他还指出了几起他认为法律工作马虎的例子，并威胁要对联邦检察官实施制裁，因为他们未能遵守法律并在签署的文件中误导了法院。

contentParagraphs.en: The case highlighted the Trump administration's ongoing assault on press rights and the free flow of information to the public. It also provided another glimpse of the White House's eagerness to assert an expansive definition of executive authority, as prosecutors argued the U.S. attorney general has the power to set aside legal protections for journalists when he deems necessary.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该案凸显了特朗普政府对新闻权利和信息向公众自由流动的持续侵犯。这也让人们再次看到白宫渴望对行政权力进行广泛的定义，因为检察官认为，美国司法部长有权在他认为必要时取消对记者的法律保护。

contentParagraphs.en: Judge: One way or another, the subpoenas would be canceled
contentParagraphs.zh: 法官：无论如何，传票将被取消

contentParagraphs.en: All the same, at the Thursday afternoon hearing in Manhattan, the Justice Department's legal team yielded to the inescapable logic of the moment: Subramanian had declared that if the subpoenas to the Times journalists were not withdrawn, he would quash them.
contentParagraphs.zh: 尽管如此，在周四下午在曼哈顿举行的听证会上，司法部的法律团队屈服于当时不可避免的逻辑：萨勃拉曼尼亚宣称，如果不撤回对《泰晤士报》记者的传票，他将撤销这些传票。

contentParagraphs.en: "It's a great day for the First Amendment and a great day for the rule of law," David McGraw, the lead newsroom lawyer for the New York Times, told NPR moments after the court hearing wrapped up. "Once again, the federal courts have stood up for what's right in this country. We hope that's the end of it."
contentParagraphs.zh: “对于第一修正案来说，这是伟大的一天，对于法治来说也是伟大的一天，”《纽约时报》新闻编辑部首席律师戴维·麦格劳 (David McGraw) 在法庭听证会结束后不久告诉 NPR。 “联邦法院再一次捍卫了这个国家的正义。我们希望事情就此结束。”

contentParagraphs.en: Sean Buckley, the deputy U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York, declined to comment to NPR after the hearing. He presented the government's case in court Thursday and told the presiding judge that he was leading the broader investigation into the leak of security information to the Times.
contentParagraphs.zh: 纽约南区美国副检察官肖恩·巴克利 (Sean Buckley) 在听证会后拒绝向 NPR 发表评论。他周四在法庭上陈述了政府的案子，并告诉主审法官，他正在领导对《纽约时报》泄露安全信息一事进行更广泛的调查。

contentParagraphs.en: A Justice Department spokesperson issued a statement to NPR assailing the judge's ruling and noting he threatened its attorneys with sanctions unless the subpoenas were withdrawn.
contentParagraphs.zh: 司法部发言人向全国公共广播电台发表声明，批评法官的裁决，并指出他威胁律师，除非撤回传票，否则将受到制裁。

contentParagraphs.en: "This judge's conduct overrides clear longstanding principles and common sense - blocking the grand jury from receiving core evidence in a national security investigation," the spokesperson said. "Make no mistake, this investigation remains ongoing, and we will pursue justice against those threating national security by leaking classified information, a serious federal crime."
contentParagraphs.zh: 该发言人表示：“这位法官的行为超越了明确的长期原则和常识——阻止大陪审团获得国家安全调查的核心证据。” “毫无疑问，这项调查仍在进行中，我们将对那些通过泄露机密信息威胁国家安全的人追究正义，这是一种严重的联邦犯罪。”

contentParagraphs.en: In the court hearing, which lasted well over an hour, Judge Subramanian laced into Buckley's logic for issuing the subpoenas just two days after the first Times article reported that Trump had left the NATO summit in Turkey on an older plane because of security concerns. A story the next day detailed many of the specific concerns.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在持续一个多小时的法庭听证会上，萨勃拉曼尼安法官解释了巴克利发出传票的逻辑，就在《泰晤士报》第一篇文章报道特朗普出于安全考虑乘坐一架旧飞机离开土耳其北约峰会两天后。第二天的一篇报道详细介绍了许多具体问题。

contentParagraphs.en: The government offered to hold the subpoenas "in abeyance" for 14 days as it pursued other investigative avenues, Buckley told the court.
contentParagraphs.zh: 巴克利告诉法庭，政府提出将传票“暂停”14 天，同时寻求其他调查途径。

contentParagraphs.en: But that held little appeal for Subramanian.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但这对萨勃拉曼尼亚来说没有什么吸引力。

contentParagraphs.en: Seeking testimony and materials from reporters as the federal government did is supposed to be a last resort, not a first move, the judge noted. He pointed to binding legal precedent as well as the Justice Department's own current policy. How, Subramanian asked again and again, could it be a last resort if done just two days after publication?
contentParagraphs.zh: 法官指出，像联邦政府那样向记者寻求证词和材料应该是最后的手段，而不是第一步。他指出具有约束力的法律先例以及司法部自己的现行政策。萨勃拉曼尼亚一次又一次地问，如果在出版后两天就这样做，这会是最后的手段吗？

contentParagraphs.en: Buckley said it was a last resort given what they knew at the time.
contentParagraphs.zh: 巴克利表示，鉴于他们当时所知道的情况，这是最后的手段。

contentParagraphs.en: The judge didn't buy that logic.
contentParagraphs.zh: 法官并不认同这种逻辑。

contentParagraphs.en: He later asked why Buckley did not know of the specific legal precedent protecting journalists prior to issuing the subpoenas as part of the investigation. That legal precedent was established in another case two decades ago that also involved The New York Times in the appeals circuit that includes New York.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他后来问巴克利为什么在作为调查的一部分发出传票之前不知道保护记者的具体法律先例。这一法律先例是在二十年前的另一起案件中确立的，该案件也将《纽约时报》纳入了包括纽约在内的上诉巡回法院。

contentParagraphs.en: "I don't review every piece of paper," Buckley responded.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我不会审阅每一篇论文，”巴克利回答道。

contentParagraphs.en: Federal prosecutor says Attorney General can override journalistic protections
contentParagraphs.zh: 联邦检察官称司法部长可以推翻新闻保护

contentParagraphs.en: But he said that the subpoenas were properly authorized by the attorney general — a reference to Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche. When they are approved by the attorney general, they can supersede any such protections, Buckley said, though he would not stipulate whether Blanche based his approval on national security, a threat to human life, or some other grounds.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但他表示，传票得到了司法部长的适当授权——指的是美国代理司法部长托德·布兰奇。巴克利说，当它们得到司法部长的批准后，它们可以取代任何此类保护措施，尽管他没有说明布兰奇的批准是否基于国家安全、对人类生命的威胁或其他一些理由。

contentParagraphs.en: David McCraw, right, general counsel for the New York Times, and Douglas Zolkind, left, attorney for the New York Times, leave Manhattan federal court, Thursday, July 23, 2026, in New York.
contentParagraphs.zh: 《纽约时报》总法律顾问大卫·麦克劳（右）和《纽约时报》律师道格拉斯·佐尔金德（左）于 2026 年 7 月 23 日星期四离开纽约曼哈顿联邦法院。

contentParagraphs.en: In the polite language of the courtroom, Subramanian patiently upbraided prosecutors for asking another federal judge to authorize an order bidding mobile phone providers not to tell the journalists that their phone records had been subpoenaed — days after the Justice Department had already publicly confirmed the existence of the first round of subpoenas.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在法庭上，萨勃拉曼尼亚用礼貌的语言耐心地谴责检察官要求另一位联邦法官授权招标移动电话提供商的命令，不要告诉记者他们的电话记录已被传唤——几天前，司法部已经公开证实了第一轮传票的存在。

contentParagraphs.en: A federal attorney had said that telling the reporters of the demand for phone records would compromise the investigation by disclosing its existence — even though it was already known publicly and widely reported by the Times, NPR and other outlets.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一名联邦检察官曾表示，告诉记者索取电话记录的要求将会因为披露电话记录的存在而损害调查——尽管这一事件已经被公众所知，并被《泰晤士报》、美国国家公共广播电台和其他媒体广泛报道。

contentParagraphs.en: Called forward by the judge, the attorney came forward and said it was an unintended mistake.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在法官的传唤下，律师出面表示这是一个无意的错误。

articles.title: Scientists find the secret of birdsong's 'spectacular diversity'
articles.summary: A scientific study has revealed the acoustic secret that connects all birdsong - and it is surprisingly simple
articles.titleZh: 科学家发现鸟鸣声“壮观多样性”的秘密
articles.summaryZh: 一项科学研究揭示了连接所有鸟鸣的声音秘密 - 而且它非常简单

contentParagraphs.en: Scientists find the secret of birdsong's 'spectacular diversity'Image source, Clément Cornec/ENES Bioacoustics Research LabImage caption, The songs of birds like this European wren are shaped by their environmentByVictoria GillScience correspondent Scientists in France say they have discovered the building blocks of the incredible diversity of birdsong.A team analysed more than100,000 songs from 3,000 bird species and revealed that they are all composed of eight fundamental types of sound that the researchers call "motifs". Each song, they say, is made up of combinations of these motifs - three types of trill, three types of whistle, chaotic notes and harmonies.Birdsong is one of the natural world's most sophisticated forms of sound-based communication. Figure caption, Listen to the eight acoustic building blocks of birdsongIts variety and complexity has fascinated biologists, but with so many different songs, it has been difficult to study on a global scale.Lead researcher and University of Saint-Etienne PhD student Quentin Bacquelé explained: "The diversity is huge. And if you go to Asia, Africa, Amazonia, you will hear totally different songs."In a new study, he and his colleagues used a global database of birdsong - recordings uploaded by thousands of volunteers. This meant they could analyse about 116,000 songs from more than 3,000 birdsong species.They studied patterns in the songs - how each song shifted and changed through time. And with the help of AI, they could then compare the thousands of songs to each other - to find what connected them. The scientists said this unveiled "a simplicity underlying the spectacular diversity of birdsongs".Image source, Chester ZooImage caption, The tropical Javan green magpie is critically endangeredA key finding was that the environment the birds evolved in - from tropical forest to temperate landscape - shaped how they "composed" their songs. So birds adapted for tropical forests use different acoustic building blocks, or motifs, to those from temperate regions.Simpler motifs, like flat whistles, Bacquelé explained, are often found in tropical environments, apparently because they help the birds make themselves heard through the busy, biodiverse soundscape and dense vegetation.More complex compositions - with ultrafast trills and combinations that contain lots of information - are more common in temperate forests, where birds tend to communicate at closer range.It's a kind of evolutionary trade-off: A very complex song provides a lot of information, but it is difficult to transmit that information over long distances in complex environments.Image source, Vincent Médoc, ENES Bioacoustics Research LabImage caption, A three-wattled bellbird, a bird from the Central American rainforest whose song carries over long distancesThis link between the environment and its natural soundscape, Bacquelé explained, is being transformed by human activity. "I think this research has opened up a new way to see songs as something [intrinsically] linked with the environment," he told BBC News. "So, we could try to use these motifs to help us understand how birds could adapt to the new background noise [that we produce]."Natural sound, Bacquelé added, is an an important part of our own connection to nature and is worth conserving. Image source, Victoria GillImage caption, A nuthatch perches in a tree in northern England "If you think of a forest, near your town or near your house, you think about the songs of the birds in the morning when you go outside."And if a bird in a forest has a specific song, if you destroy the forest, then that song may not be adapted any more to the new environment."The study is published in the journal Science, external.
contentParagraphs.zh: 科学家发现了鸟鸣“壮观多样性”的秘密 图片来源，Clément Cornec/ENES 生物声学研究实验室 图片说明，像欧洲鹪鹩这样的鸟类的鸣叫是由它们的环境塑造的 作者：Victoria Gill 科学通讯员 法国科学家表示，他们已经发现了鸟鸣令人难以置信的多样性的组成部分。一个团队分析了 3,000 种鸟类的 100,000 多首鸣叫声，发现它们全部由研究人员研究的八种基本声音类型组成称之为“主题”。他们说，每首歌都是由这些主题的组合组成的——三种类型的颤音、三种类型的口哨、混乱的音符和和声。鸟鸣是自然界最复杂的声音交流形式之一。图标题，聆听鸟鸣的八个声音组成部分它的多样性和复杂性让生物学家着迷，但由于鸟鸣的种类如此之多，因此很难在全球范围内进行研究。首席研究员、圣艾蒂安大学博士生 Quentin Bacquelé 解释说：“多样性是巨大的。如果你去亚洲、非洲、亚马逊流域，你会听到完全不同的鸣叫。”在一项新的研究中，他和他的同事使用了一个全球鸟鸣数据库 - 由数千人上传的录音的志愿者。这意味着他们可以分析来自 3,000 多种鸟鸣物种的约 116,000 首歌曲。他们研究了歌曲的模式 - 每首歌曲如何随时间变化和变化。在人工智能的帮助下，他们可以将数千首歌曲相互进行比较，找出它们之间的联系。科学家们表示，这揭示了“鸟类鸣叫的壮观多样性背后的简单性”。图片来源，切斯特动物园图片说明，热带爪哇绿喜鹊极度濒临灭绝一项重要发现是，鸟类进化的环境——从热带森林到温带景观——塑造了它们“创作”歌曲的方式。因此，适应热带森林的鸟类使用与温带地区不同的声学构件或主题。巴克莱解释说，更简单的主题，如扁平的口哨，经常出现在热带环境中，显然是因为它们可以帮助鸟类在繁忙、生物多样性的音景和茂密的植被中听到自己的声音。更复杂的作品——超快的颤音和包含大量信息的组合——在温带森林中更常见，那里的鸟类倾向于在更近距离的范围内交流。这是一种进化权衡：一首非常复杂的歌曲提供了大量信息，但在复杂的环境中很难长距离传输这些信息。图片来源，Vincent Médoc，ENES 生物声学研究实验室图片说明，三垂钟鸟，一种来自中美洲雨林的鸟，其歌声可以远距离传播。巴克尔解释说，环境与其自然音景之间的这种联系正在被人类活动所改变。 “我认为这项研究开辟了一种新的方式，将歌曲视为与环境[内在]相关的东西，”他告诉英国广播公司新闻。 “因此，我们可以尝试利用这些主题来帮助我们了解鸟类如何适应[我们产生的]新背景噪音。”巴克莱补充道，自然声音是我们与自然联系的重要组成部分，值得保护。图片来源，Victoria Gill 图片说明，一只五子雀栖息在英格兰北部的一棵树上“如果你想到一座森林，靠近你的城镇或你的房子，你就会想到早上出门时鸟儿的歌声。”如果森林里的鸟儿有一首特定的歌声，如果你破坏了森林，那么这首歌可能不再适应新的环境。“这项研究发表在《科学》杂志上，外部。

articles.title: France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
articles.summary: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula.
articles.titleZh: 法国下令旅游景点疏散，数百人乘船逃离野火
articles.summaryZh: 大火已经在该地区肆虐了好几天，官员们现在下令整个费雷角半岛的人员撤离。

contentParagraphs.en: French authorities have ordered the evacuation of the entirety of the Cap Ferret peninsula on the south-west coast, with hundreds of people escaping the area by boat.
contentParagraphs.zh: 法国当局已下令西南海岸整个费雷角半岛撤离，数百人乘船逃离该地区。

contentParagraphs.en: Tens of thousands of people are being evacuated from the tourism destination west of Bordeaux, as firefighters continue to battle wildfires that have raged in the Gironde region for days.The fires, which began on Tuesday in Saumos, continued to burn overnight with 8,700 hectares (around 21,500 acres) consumed by the flames.Meanwhile, the government in Spain has declared a national emergency as wildfires burn out of control in several areas close to the capital Madrid.Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez spoke of a "dramatic situation" and urged people to take great care. Three fires were burning west of Madrid, at Villa del Prado and San Martín de Valdeiglesias.French Interior Minister Laurent Nuñez said 40,000 people had either been or were in the process of being evacuated from the Cap Ferret peninsula, which has the Atlantic on one side and the Bay of Arcachon on the other."A total of 80 homes have burnt, around 50 of which have been completely destroyed," he told reporters.The mayor of Arès, which lies south-east of Lège-Cap Ferret on the Bay of Arcachon, said his town was being evacuated too. A local councillor said 2,000 evacuees from the peninsula had already arrived by boat in the port of Arcachon on the south side of the bay.Further down the Atlantic coast, around the town of Biscarosse in France's Landes region, another major fire that began on Thursday afternoon has prompted the evacuations of more than 23,000 people.About 18,000 people in Biscarrosse and 5,000 in Parentis-en-Born had to leave their homes, campsites, a nursing home and a summer camp.No further casualties have been reported overnight in south-west France, although two firefighters died on Tuesday while tackling a blaze near Bordeaux airport.French President Emmanuel Macron said on Thursday night the wildfire situation in the country was "very tense" - especially in the Gironde region.Macron said he had asked for help from the European Union, and that support would soon come in the form of aircraft from Croatia, Portugal, the Czech Republic and Slovakia.Gironde prefect Sophie Brocas ordered the "final phase" of the peninsula evacuation early on Friday morning.People are being told to leave via the main D106 road towards Bordeaux while boats were being made available for others between 07:00 and 10:00 local time (06:00 BST and 09:00 BST) from four piers.The road runs along the east side of the peninsula and is the only land route out.
contentParagraphs.zh: 数万人正在从波尔多以西的旅游目的地疏散，消防队员仍在继续与吉伦特省肆虐多日的野火作斗争。周二在索莫斯开始的大火持续一夜燃烧，大火烧毁了8,700公顷（约21,500英亩）土地。与此同时，西班牙政府已宣布全国进入紧急状态，因为靠近波尔多地区的多个地区野火失控。首都马德里。西班牙首相佩德罗·桑切斯谈到了“戏剧性的情况”，并敦促人们高度小心。马德里以西的普拉多别墅和圣马丁德巴尔代格莱西亚斯发生了三起火灾。法国内政部长洛朗·努涅斯表示，费雷角半岛一侧是大西洋，另一侧是阿卡雄湾，有 4 万人已经或正在撤离。“共有 80 所房屋被烧毁，其中约 50 所已被完全摧毁，”他告诉记者。位于阿卡雄湾莱日角费雷东南部的阿雷斯市市长表示，他所在的城镇也正在被疏散。一名当地议员表示，2000名从半岛撤离的人员已经乘船抵达海湾南侧的阿卡雄港。在大西洋沿岸，法国朗德地区比斯卡罗斯镇周围，周四下午开始的另一场大火已促使超过23,000人疏散。比斯卡罗斯约有18,000人，法国约5,000人被疏散。博恩地区帕兰蒂斯不得不离开家园、露营地、疗养院和夏令营。法国西南部一夜之间没有进一步的人员伤亡报告，不过周二有两名消防员在波尔多机场附近扑灭大火时死亡。法国总统埃马纽埃尔·马克龙周四晚表示，该国的山火局势“非常紧张”，尤其是吉伦特省。马克龙表示，他已向欧盟寻求帮助，很快就会以飞机的形式提供支持来自克罗地亚、葡萄牙、捷克共和国和斯洛伐克。吉伦特省省长索菲·布罗卡斯 (Sophie Brocas) 于周五一早下令进行半岛疏散的“最后阶段”。人们被告知通过 D106 主干道前往波尔多，同时在当地时间 07:00 至 10:00（英国夏令时 06:00 和英国夏令时间 09:00）从四个码头为其他人提供船只。道路沿东侧延伸是半岛的唯一陆路出境通道。

articles.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
articles.summary: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear.
articles.titleZh: 人工智能支出扰乱市场，谷歌和特斯拉股价暴跌
articles.summaryZh: 随着各大科技公司竞相利用人工智能技术的新浪潮，投资者想知道何时会出现经济效益。

contentParagraphs.en: Shares of Google and Tesla plunged on Thursday as investors were spooked by the ever-increasing amounts of money being spent on artificial intelligence (AI).Google's parent company Alphabet saw its share price drop by nearly 7%, while Elon Musk's electric vehicle-maker Tesla saw its stock fall 14.5%.Both reported negative free cash flow - the money retained after paying for operations and investments - in financial results on Wednesday, alongside promises to spend billions more in the months and years to come.It was the first time Google had seen the cash metric turn negative since it became a public company in 2004, according to its financial records.As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors are wondering when financial benefits will show up. Alphabet now expects to spend as much as $205bn this year, mainly on AI projects and infrastructure, a $15bn increase from a spending estimate it gave just three months ago. Meanwhile, Tesla expects this year to spend up to $25bn on unspecified projects."There is still a healthy degree of scepticism about the ability of these investments to generate a commensurate level of return," Russ Mould, an investment director at AJ Bell, said. Alphabet's combined quarterly revenue hit $119.8bn, up 23% compared with the same period last year.Though Google parent Alphabet saw its business continue to grow in recent months, heavy spending on artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure pushed its leftover cash into negative territory.The company's free cash flow came in at negative $5.9bn (£4.3bn) for the first time in at least a decade, according to its past financial records.Its stock quickly fell 4% in after-hours trading.Anat Ashkanazi, Google's chief financial officer, noted on a call with financial analysts that the company had recorded negative free cash flow due to growing capital expenditures, essentially all of which were related to AI spending.She said the company spent $45bn in the second quarter, with 60% of the cost going towards servers and the remaining 40% going towards data centres. Alphabet's capital spending was $36bn in the first quarter of this year.Ashkanazi said on the call that when it comes to AI, "the demand still outpaces that investment". "As long as we see these attractive opportunities to invest, we will continue to invest."Sundar Pichai, Google's chief executive, said the technological shift to AI tools and capabilities still "feels like early innings in a shift across multiple areas" and the company's plans around generating financial returns on its spending were "disciplined". "What I see with what you can do with frontier capabilities, there is still a lot of work left to do to translate that into experiences for our users. So that looks like extraordinary opportunities with extraordinary returns."Rachel Winter, a partner at the wealth management firm Killik & Co, said there was some surprise among investors about how much Google was spending."These are huge numbers. The fact that the shares dropped when the results came out, that suggests there is a little bit of concern about those levels."Tesla reported negative free cash flow on Wednesday of $1.1bn for the second quarter due to its own increasing investment costs.It was the company's first negative showing of leftover cash in two years, according to its financial records.Tesla's plans to spend $25bn this year is more than double its capital spending in 2025.Vaibhav Taneja, Tesla's chief financial officer, said on Wednesday that Tesla was in "a big investment cycle" and its spending would probably increase further over the next three years.
contentParagraphs.zh: 谷歌和特斯拉的股价周四暴跌，投资者对人工智能 (AI) 上不断增加的资金投入感到震惊。谷歌母公司 Alphabet 的股价下跌了近 7%，而埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 的电动汽车制造商特斯拉的股价下跌了 14.5%。周三，两家公司在财务业绩中都报告了负自由现金流（支付运营和投资后保留的资金），并承诺在未来数月和数年增加数十亿美元的支出。根据其财务记录，谷歌自 2004 年成为上市公司以来，现金指标首次出现负数。 随着各大科技公司竞相利用新一波的人工智能技术，投资者想知道财务收益何时会显现出来。 Alphabet 目前预计今年支出高达 2050 亿美元，主要用于人工智能项目和基础设施，比三个月前给出的支出预估增加了 150 亿美元。与此同时，特斯拉预计今年将在未具体说明的项目上花费高达 250 亿美元。AJ Bell 投资总监 Russ Mould 表示，“人们对这些投资能否产生相应水平的回报仍存在一定程度的怀疑”。 Alphabet 的季度总收入达到 1198 亿美元，与去年同期相比增长 23%。尽管谷歌母公司 Alphabet 的业务近几个月来持续增长，但人工智能 (AI) 基础设施的大量支出将其剩余现金推向负值。根据过去的财务记录，该公司的自由现金流至少十年来首次出现负 59 亿美元（合 43 亿英镑）。其股价在盘后交易后迅速下跌 4%谷歌首席财务官阿纳特·阿什卡纳齐(Anat Ashkanazi)在与财务分析师的电话会议上指出，由于资本支出不断增长，该公司自由现金流出现负值，基本上所有这些支出都与人工智能支出有关。她表示，该公司第二季度支出450亿美元，其中60%的成本用于服务器，其余40%用于数据中心。 Alphabet 今年第一季度的资本支出为 360 亿美元。 Ashkanazi 在电话会议上表示，在人工智能方面，“需求仍然超过投资”。 “只要我们看到这些有吸引力的投资机会，我们就会继续投资。”谷歌首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊(Sundar Pichai)表示，向人工智能工具和功能的技术转变“感觉像是跨多个领域转变的早期阶段”，而且该公司围绕其支出产生财务回报的计划是“有纪律的”。财富管理公司 Killik & Co 的合伙人雷切尔·温特 (Rachel Winter) 表示，投资者对谷歌的支出感到有些惊讶。“这些数字很大。业绩公布时股价下跌，这表明人们对这些水平有些担忧。”由于自身投资成本不断增加，第二季度剩余现金为11亿美元。根据其财务记录，这是该公司两年来首次出现剩余现金负数。特斯拉今年计划支出250亿美元，是2025年资本支出的两倍多。特斯拉首席财务官瓦伊巴夫·塔内贾(Vaibhav Taneja)周三表示，特斯拉正处于“一个大投资周期”，未来三年其支出可能会进一步增加。

articles.title: UK complacent about war threat, warns BAE boss
articles.summary:
The threat of attack has never been higher, the boss of Europe's biggest defence contractor has warned.
Dr Charles Woodburn
articles.titleZh: 英国航空航天公司（BAE）老板警告说，英国对战争威胁感到自满
articles.summaryZh:
欧洲最大国防承包商的老板警告说，攻击的威胁从未如此之高。
查尔斯·伍德伯恩博士

contentParagraphs.en: Image source, iStockBySimon JackBusiness editorThe UK has been too complacent about the risk of foreign attack, the boss of Europe's biggest defence contractor BAE Systems has warned.Dr Charles Woodburn told the BBC in an exclusive interview that the level of threat was the highest he'd seen in his lifetime.He welcomed the recent boost to defence spending but said much more would be needed to hit the government's commitment to spend 3.5% of GDP on defence by 2035.He also warned we were not far from the use of autonomous lethal weapons by countries that may not follow the UK's policy of maintaining human control.In a rare interview, Woodburn told the BBC the reality of modern warfare had "changed quite profoundly" and that UK defence spending had to change with it.He said Russia and Ukraine had become "incredibly adept" at using autonomy in the form of drones and counter drones."It's something that we now have to really understand and make sure that we're able to provide the capabilities that can counter that and deter aggression," he said.Image source, Getty ImagesImage caption, BAE Systems unveiled a model of Brontanax, an uncrewed Collaborative Combat Aircraft at Farnborough International AirshowBAE Systems unveiled a life-size model of an unmanned fighter jet in front of the new Defence Secretary Wes Streeting at the Farnborough International Air Show.Four of these so-called Collaborative Combat Aircraft will accompany a manned fighter to massively increase firepower but at just 20% of the cost of a piloted aircraft.However, Woodburn said there was still a role for the traditional battleships and submarines that BAE has been manufacturing for decades."If you look at what our adversaries are building, they're also building significantly large platforms - aircraft, ships, submarines. And they're supplemented by some of these unmanned capabilities." It is the combination of the two that has the "winning" formula, he said.It is hardly surprising that the boss of a defence company would like to see more defence spending. But Woodburn said the UK had been complacent for some time about the level of threat it faced - particularly from Russia."This is by far the most threatening time I've seen," he said. "Even when I joined the company in 2016 I was amazed at what we saw Russian submarines doing, the cyber attacks. I think that's come a long way sadly as a result of the Ukraine conflict we're much more aware of the proximity of a hot war in Europe."Image source, Getty ImagesImage caption, Dr Charles Woodburn CEO of BAE Systems, speaks at Farnborough International AirshowThe war readiness of the UK's armed forces has been questioned by senior armed services figures and openly mocked by the US. General Sir Gwyn Jenkins, the First Sea Lord, recently said the navy is not ready for war. Pete Hegseth, the US defence secretary sarcastically referred to the "big bad Royal Navy" while President Donald Trump described the UK's aircraft carriers as "toys".That impression was not helped by the tardiness of mobilising the destroyer HMS Dragon – built by BAE - to provide air cover for Cyprus against Iranian and Hezbollah threats.Woodburn insisted the company had responded as soon as the Ministry of Defence had asked for its help.He said BAE gets "things in place as quickly as we're able to but at the end of the day you need a signal to move".Image source, Getty ImagesImage caption, Former Defence Secretary John Healey has been made ChancellorWoodburn welcomed the promotion of former Defence Secretary John Healey to chancellor. Healey very publicly resigned from the post insisting that the extra money awarded to defence "falls well short of what is needed to protect the country".An additional £13.5bn was boosted to an extra £15bn after he resigned but that will only bring spending on defence to 2.68% of GDP by 2030 – well short of the 3% Healey said was required. A further massive increase in spending will be needed to hit the government goal of 3.5% by 2035.Shares in defence companies – including BAE – rose on the news that Healey was moving into number 11 but Woodburn knows he will have other departments competing for cash."We have tremendous respect for John," he said. "He understands what is needed and I think in that sense its very good he's in the Treasury role."He recognised Healey would have a host of complicated national finances to tackle. "But the fact that he understands the requirements of defence we see as a very positive thing", he said.The UK's armed forces have dwindled since the end of the cold war. In 1990 – the army had 153,000 regular soldiers. Today that number is just 74,000.But it is debatable how many human soldiers you need these days and Woodburn said a new era of autonomous lethal weaponry was close at hand."We're not far from that. While we follow rules of 'man in the loop' we know very well that our adversaries are not always doing that," he said.Image source, Getty ImagesImage caption, A video message by UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham was played prior a speech by Defence Secretary Wes Streeting during the Farnborough International Air ShowAt the air show, new UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham sent a video with a message that defence could play a big role in his plans to reindustrialise the economy and provide thousands of skilled jobs.And in his keynote speech at the airshow Streeting said: "Defence will not just be a recipient of the proceeds of economic growth. It will be a central contributor to building that growth, a driver of prosperity in parts of the country that are crying out for the backing they need to make their contribution to the challenge of our time."Woodburn said he wanted to help make the case for the knock-on benefits of defence spending."We need to make sure that the industrial benefits, the jobs benefits are well told so people understand this is money well spent," he said.But does he ever lose sleep over the fact that his products are ultimately intended to
contentParagraphs.zh: 图片来源，iStockBySimon JackBusiness 编辑欧洲最大的国防承包商 BAE Systems 的老板警告说，英国对外国攻击的风险过于自满。查尔斯·伍德伯恩博士在接受 BBC 独家采访时表示，威胁程度是他一生中见过的最高水平。他对最近国防开支的增加表示欢迎，但表示要兑现政府到 2035 年将 GDP 的 3.5% 用于国防的承诺，还需要付出更多努力。他还警告说，我们并没有做到这一点。远离英国维持人类控制政策的国家使用自主致命武器。在一次罕见的采访中，伍德伯恩告诉英国广播公司，现代战争的现实已经“发生了相当深刻的变化”，英国的国防开支也必须随之改变。他说，俄罗斯和乌克兰已经“非常擅长”以无人机和反无人机的形式使用自主权。“这是我们现在必须真正理解的事情，并确保我们能够提供能够对抗这种情况的能力，他说：“阻止侵略。”图片来源，盖蒂图片社 图片说明，BAE系统公司在范堡罗国际航展上展示了Brontanax的模型，这是一架无人协作战斗机。BAE系统公司在范堡罗国际航展上，在新任国防部长韦斯·斯特里廷面前展示了一架真人大小的无人驾驶战斗机模型。其中四架所谓的协作战斗机将伴随一架载人战斗机，以大幅增加火力，但无人驾驶飞机成本的 20%。不过，伍德伯恩表示，BAE 几十年来制造的传统战列舰和潜艇仍然发挥着作用。“如果你看看我们的对手正在建造的东西，他们也在建造非常大的平台——飞机、舰艇、潜艇。而且它们还辅以一些无人能力。”他说，这两者的结合才是“制胜”的秘诀。 一家国防公司的老板希望看到更多的国防开支，这并不奇怪。但伍德伯恩表示，一段时间以来，英国对其面临的威胁程度感到自满，尤其是来自俄罗斯的威胁。“这是迄今为止我所见过的最具威胁的时刻，”他说。 “即使我在 2016 年加入该公司，我也对我们看到的俄罗斯潜艇所做的网络攻击感到惊讶。遗憾的是，由于乌克兰冲突，我们更加意识到欧洲即将爆发热战。”遭到美国的公开嘲讽。第一海务大臣格温·詹金斯将军最近表示，海军尚未做好战争准备。美国国防部长皮特·赫格斯 (Pete Hegseth) 讽刺地提到“大坏蛋皇家海军”，而唐纳德·特朗普 (Donald Trump) 总统则将英国的航空母舰描述为“玩具”。英国航空航天公司 (BAE) 建造的驱逐舰“龙”号驱逐舰为塞浦路斯提供空中掩护，抵御伊朗和真主党的威胁，而迟迟没有调动这种印象，这无助于这种印象。伍德伯恩坚称，英国国防部一请求帮助，该公司就立即做出了回应。我们会尽快行动，但最终你需要一个信号来行动”。 图片来源，盖蒂图片社 图片说明，前国防部长约翰·希利已被任命为总理伍德伯恩欢迎前国防部长约翰·希利晋升为总理。希利非常公开地辞去职务，坚称用于国防的额外资金“远远低于保护国家所需的资金”。在他辞职后，额外的135亿英镑增加到额外的150亿英镑，但这只会使到2030年国防支出占GDP的2.68%——远低于希利所说的3%的要求。到 2035 年，需要进一步大幅增加支出，以达到政府 3.5% 的目标。希利跻身第 11 位的消息传出后，包括 BAE 在内的国防公司股价上涨，但伍德伯恩知道他将让其他部门争夺现金。“我们非常尊重约翰，”他说。 “他了解需要什么，我认为从这个意义上说，他担任财政部的角色非常好。”他认识到希利将有许多复杂的国家财政问题需要解决。他说：“但事实上，他了解国防的要求，我们认为这是一件非常积极的事情。”自冷战结束以来，英国的武装部队一直在减少。 1990年——军队有正规军153,000人。如今这个数字只有 74,000 人。但现在需要多少人类士兵是有争议的，伍德伯恩表示，自主致命武器的新时代即将到来。“我们离这个目标并不远。虽然我们遵循‘人在循环’的规则，但我们很清楚我们的对手并不总是这样做。”他说。图片来源，盖蒂图片社 图片说明，在国防部长韦斯·斯特里廷发表讲话之前播放了英国首相安迪·伯纳姆的视频致辞在范堡罗国际航展期间，英国新任首相安迪·伯纳姆发送了一段视频，传达了这样的信息：国防可以在他的经济再工业化计划中发挥重要作用，并提供数千个熟练工作岗位。斯特里廷在航展的主题演讲中表示：“国防将不仅仅是经济增长收益的接受者。它将成为建设经济增长的核心贡献者，成为国家部分地区繁荣的推动者，这些地区迫切需要他们做出贡献所需的支持。” “我们需要确保工业利益、就业利益得到充分宣传，以便人们明白这笔钱花得值。”伍德伯恩说，他想帮助证明国防开支的连锁效益。但他是否曾因为他的产品最终旨在

articles.title: Egyptian warrior princesses, ancient avocados, and more science news
articles.summary: Hannah Chin and Nate Rott of NPR's Short Wave podcast talk about the building blocks of birdsong, the evolutionary history of avocados and Egyptian warrior princesses.
articles.titleZh: 埃及武士公主、古代鳄梨和更多科学新闻
articles.summaryZh: NPR 短波播客的汉娜·钦 (Hannah Chin) 和内特·罗特 (Nate Rott) 谈论鸟鸣的组成部分、牛油果的进化史和埃及武士公主。

contentParagraphs.en: AILSA CHANG, HOST: It's time now for our science news roundup from Short Wave, NPR's science podcast. I'm joined now by Hannah Chinn and Nate Rott. Hey to both of you.NATE ROTT, BYLINE: Hello.HANNAH CHINN, BYLINE: Hey, hey.CHANG: Hello, hello. OK. So, as always, you have brought us three science stories that caught your attention recently. Tell me about them.ROTT: Yeah. So the first is a discovery about ancient avocados that...CHANG: What?ROTT: ...Ailsa, could help keep guacamole on your list of snacks.CHANG: That will never leave my list of snacks.(LAUGHTER)CHINN: So the next one looks at the mummified remains of Egyptian princesses and upends some of our assumptions about how they lived.ROTT: And the last study takes a global look at birdsong. And trust me, Ailsa, it's going to be trilling. See what I did there?CHANG: (Laughter) I see what you did there, Nate. All right. So let's start with these ancient avocados - never knew that they were ancient or could have been. Is my avocado toast in danger here?CHINN: Well, not yet. So when you pick up avocados at the grocery store, or when you get guac in your burrito, chances are that it's from a single type of avocado. It's called the Hass avocado.CHANG: Right.CHINN: And Ailsa, this type of avocado makes up more than 80% of commercial avocado production.ROTT: Which is, you know, kind of convenient because it means that our avocados tend to be, like, consistent in their size, shape, flavor-wise. But because they're all genetically identical, they're all susceptible to diseases in the exact same way.CHANG: Oh. Like farmers are putting all their eggs - ha-ha, or avocados, shall we say - in one basket here? So are there other varieties of avocados that we could grow so there's sort of, like, genetic variation?CHINN: Yeah. There are. And a lot of them look very different.KEVIN WANN: They're bigger. They're wider (ph). They're glossier, and on the inside, they tend to be less fatty.CHANG: Well, that's not as tasty.(LAUGHTER)CHINN: Well, but they're more genetically diverse. So this is archaeobotanist Kevin Wann. He's a fellow at the Smithsonian Museum of Natural History and a PhD candidate at Texas A&M doing avocado research.ROTT: But what Kevin wanted to know was how much genetic diversity he was dealing with. So he collected DNA samples from a bunch of different avocado varieties in Mexico and Central America, mostly from local farmers or home gardens.CHINN: Yeah. And he sequenced the genomes of these avocados to compare them to each other and also to an existing archive of avocado varieties. Kind of like a 23andMe or Ancestry.com test but for avocados.CHANG: That's kind of cute. OK. So what did they find?ROTT: So he found a lot of genetic diversity and a few varieties of Nicaraguan avocados that were super genetically similar to those in South America, which suggests they were brought there by ancient farmers and then hybridized...CHANG: Oh.ROTT: ...With native species. Kevin published a paper on these findings this past week in the scientific journal Plants, People, Planet.CHINN: Then I talked to anthropologist Amber VanDerwarker. She's at UC Santa Barbara, and she wasn't involved with the study. But she said Kevin's research really reveals this genetic diversity that has been preserved over the past thousands of years by these intrepid, ancient farmers. And she told me it's important for us to keep this diversity alive - right? - so avocados stay sustainable into the future.CHANG: OK. So let's move on to Egyptian princesses. Tell me more.CHINN: Yeah. So maybe what I love most about this story is that it came from an effort to unclutter a basement.ROTT: (Laughter) Yeah. So that's what The Egyptian Museum in Cairo was doing when they rediscovered this box with the mummified remains of six members of an ancient Egyptian royal family that had been excavated and stashed more than a hundred years ago.CHANG: Yeah. Totally normal thing to find in one's basement.ROTT: Of course.CHANG: So who was this family?CHINN: It was five princesses and a king...CHANG: Wow.CHINN: ...Who lived thousands of years ago, around 1800 B.C.CHANG: Wow. A girl dad.CHINN: Yeah. Exactly.ROTT: Yeah. But one of the things that confused the archaeologists when they found these remains was that the women were all buried with weapons, like daggers and maces and bows and arrows, which archaeologists typically associate with men.CHANG: Of course.ROTT: So the assumption was the princesses were buried with these weapons for symbolic reasons.CHANG: Symbolic? Wait. Hello. Have these people not ever heard of "Xena: Warrior Princess"?ROTT: (Laughter).CHANG: Because women do carry weapons sometimes, OK?CHINN: Exactly. And that's what the person who helped find these remains wanted to figure out.ROTT: Yeah. Her name is Zeinab Hashesh. She's a bioarchaeologist at Beni-Suef University in Egypt. And she says, you know, we all have this assumption that princesses are like Disney characters.ZEINAB HASHESH: That they are fluffy. They are wearing beautiful dresses and very beautiful jewelry.CHINN: When Zeinab looked at these bones, she noticed that these skeletons had really strong muscle attachments.CHANG: These women were buff.CHINN: Exactly. And she did a deeper analysis of the bones, and she found these women were, yeah, buff. They were really strong.ROTT: Yeah. They were really buff. They had strong forearms, strong shoulders, and, like, some of them had curvatures in their hand bones which are likely from the repeated use of a bow and arrow.CHANG: Wow. Wait. So these women literally were warrior princesses?ROTT: Well, Zeinab says it's unclear if these findings, which are published in the journal Frontiers in Environmental Archaeology, proved that they were actually, like, warriors in the sense that they fought. That's also what I heard from another Egyptologist who wasn't involved in the study. But he says it does show that their lives weren't as relaxed as we might have thought
contentParagraphs.zh: 主持人 AILSA CHANG：现在是 NPR 科学播客短波的科学新闻综述时间。现在汉娜·钦 (Hannah Chinn) 和内特·罗特 (Nate Rott) 也加入了我的行列。嘿，你们俩。内特·罗特（NATE ROTT）连线：你好。汉娜·钦（HANNAH CHINN）连线：嘿，嘿。张：你好，你好。好的。因此，一如既往，您为我们带来了最近引起您注意的三个科学故事。告诉我关于他们的事。 ROTT：是的。所以第一个是关于古代牛油果的发现……张：什么？罗特：……艾尔莎，可以帮助你把鳄梨酱保留在你的零食清单上。张：那永远不会离开我的零食清单。（笑声）钦：所以下一个研究着眼于埃及公主的木乃伊遗骸，颠覆了我们对她们生活方式的一些假设。罗特：最后一项研究对鸟鸣进行了全球性的观察。相信我，艾尔莎，这将会是令人颤栗的。看看我在那里做了什么？ 张：（笑声）我明白你在那里做了什么，内特。好的。那么，让我们从这些古老的牛油果开始——我从来不知道它们很古老，也不知道它们可能很古老。我的牛油果吐司有危险吗？CHINN：嗯，还没有。因此，当您在杂货店买鳄梨时，或者当您在墨西哥卷饼中加入鳄梨酱时，很可能它来自单一类型的鳄梨。它被称为哈斯牛油果。CHANG：对。CHINN：艾尔莎，这种类型的牛油果占商业牛油果产量的 80% 以上。ROTT：这很方便，因为这意味着我们的牛油果在大小、形状和风味方面趋于一致。但因为它们的基因完全相同，所以它们都以完全相同的方式容易感染疾病。 张：哦。就像农民把所有的鸡蛋 - 哈哈，或者牛油果，我们可以说 - 放在一个篮子里吗？那么，我们是否可以种植其他品种的鳄梨，从而产生某种遗传变异？CHINN：是的。有。其中很多看起来都非常不同。 KEVIN WANN：它们更大。它们更宽（ph）。它们更有光泽，而且内部脂肪含量较少。CHANG：嗯，那不太好吃。（笑声）CHINN：嗯，但它们的基因更加多样化。这是考古植物学家凯文·万恩。他是史密森尼自然历史博物馆的研究员，也是德克萨斯农工大学的博士生，从事鳄梨研究。 ROTT：但凯文想知道的是他正在处理多少遗传多样性。因此，他从墨西哥和中美洲的许多不同鳄梨品种中收集了 DNA 样本，其中大部分来自当地农民或家庭花园。CHINN：是的。他对这些鳄梨的基因组进行了测序，以便将它们相互比较，并与现有的鳄梨品种档案进行比较。有点像 23andMe 或 Ancestry.com 的测试，但针对鳄梨。CHANG：这有点可爱。好的。那么他们发现了什么？罗特：所以他发现了很多遗传多样性和一些尼加拉瓜鳄梨品种，这些品种在遗传上与南美洲的鳄梨非常相似，这表明它们是由古代农民带到那里然后杂交的......张：哦。罗特：......与本地品种。凯文上周在科学杂志《植物、人类、星球》上发表了一篇关于这些发现的论文。CHINN：然后我与人类学家安布尔·范德瓦克进行了交谈。她在加州大学圣巴巴拉分校，没有参与这项研究。但她说凯文的研究确实揭示了这些勇敢的古代农民在过去数千年中保存下来的遗传多样性。她告诉我，保持这种多样性对我们来说很重要 - 对吧？ - 让鳄梨在未来保持可持续发展。 张：好的。那么让我们继续讨论埃及公主。告诉我更多。CHINN：是的。所以也许我最喜欢这个故事的是它来自于整理地下室的努力。 罗特：（笑声）是的。这就是开罗埃及博物馆在重新发现这个装有古埃及王室六名成员木乃伊遗骸的盒子时所做的事情，这些遗骸是在一百多年前被挖掘和藏起来的。 张：是的。在地下室里发现完全正常的东西。ROTT：当然。CHANG：那么这个家庭是谁？CHINN：这是五个公主和一个国王...CHANG：哇。CHINN：...谁生活在几千年前，大约公元前 1800 年。 CHANG：哇。一个女孩爸爸。CHINN：是的。完全正确。 ROTT：是的。但是，当考古学家发现这些遗骸时，让考古学家感到困惑的一件事是，这些女性都被埋葬了武器，比如匕首、狼牙棒、弓箭，考古学家通常将这些武器与男性联系在一起。 张：当然。罗特：所以我们假设公主们是出于象征性原因与这些武器一起埋葬的。张：象征性的？等待。你好。这些人难道没有听说过“西娜：武士公主”吗？ ROTT：（笑声）。 CHANG：因为女人有时确实携带武器，好吗？CHINN：没错。这就是帮助寻找这些遗骸的人想要弄清楚的。 罗特：是的。她的名字叫泽纳布·哈希什。她是埃及贝尼苏伊夫大学的生物考古学家。她说，你知道，我们都有这样的假设，即公主就像迪士尼人物。ZEINAB HASHESH：它们是毛茸茸的。他们穿着漂亮的衣服和非常漂亮的珠宝。CHINN：当 Zeinab 看着这些骨头时，她注意到这些骷髅有很强的肌肉附着力。CHANG：这些女人很强壮。CHINN：没错。她对骨头做了更深入的分析，她发现这些女人是，是的，皮肤黝黑。他们真的很强大。 ROTT：是的。他们真的很强壮。他们有强壮的前臂，强壮的肩膀，而且，有些人的手骨有弯曲，这可能是由于反复使用弓箭造成的。 张：哇。等待。那么这些女性实际上是战士公主？罗特：嗯，泽纳布说，目前还不清楚这些发表在《环境考古学前沿》杂志上的发现是否证明她们实际上是战士，就她们战斗的意义而言。这也是我从另一位没有参与这项研究的埃及古物学家那里听到的。但他说这确实表明他们的生活并不像我们想象的那么轻松

articles.title: Philippines accuses China of using water cannon on vessels for second day
articles.summary: The Philippine Coast Guard says 12 China Coast Guard vessels and three Chinese maritime militia vessels were involved.
articles.titleZh: 菲律宾指责中国连续第二天在船只上使用高压水枪
articles.summaryZh: 菲律宾海岸警卫队表示，12艘中国海警船和3艘中国海上民兵船只参与其中。

contentParagraphs.en: The Philippine Coast Guard says 12 China Coast Guard vessels and three Chinese maritime militia vessels were involved.Tensions between China and the Philippines are continuing after Manila’s coastguard accused its Chinese counterpart of firing a water cannon at government vessels near a disputed shoal for the second day.The Philippine Coast Guard said on Friday that a Chinese vessel at the Scarborough Shoal, in the Philippines’ exclusive economic zone, came within about seven metres (23 feet) of a Philippine fisheries boat, creating a “serious risk of collision”.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3AI, China, ‘dark factories’ and the future of the US auto industrylist 2 of 3How shipping insurance rates are rising, as Hormuz, Bab al-Mandeb shut downlist 3 of 3Trump imposes new double-digit tariffs on dozens of countriesend of list“The PCG strongly condemns the dangerous and unprofessional actions of the China Coast Guard,” spokesperson Jay Tarriela said in a statement.Tarriela added that the shoal was a traditional fishing ground of Filipinos, “whose rights cannot be lawfully impeded by any foreign power”.The coastguard added that 12 China Coast Guard vessels and three Chinese maritime militia vessels were involved in the incident, and water cannon hit two Philippine ships, while two others had to make dangerous manoeuvres, risking collisions.The vessels were delivering fuel subsidies to Filipino fishermen and conducting routine patrols at the Scarborough Shoal, also known to locals as Bajo de Masinloc, which lies within the Philippines’ exclusive economic zone.Earlier on Friday, the China Coast Guard said it had imposed “control measures” on several Philippine vessels it claimed were operating illegally in waters around the shoal.In a more detailed statement, it accused the Philippines of organising several government and coastguard vessels, along with a large number of fishing vessels, to “illegally gather” in the waters.It said it had taken legal measures to warn, block and deploy water cannon after the official vessels from the Philippines ignored those measures from the coast guard and “insisted on intruding.”A similar incident took place on Thursday.On Friday, China’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs said the coastguard’s actions were “reasonable, lawful ⁠and beyond reproach.”“The Philippine side should immediately stop the infringement, provocation and hype,” ministry ⁠spokesperson Lin Jian said at a regular briefing.The two incidents at Scarborough Shoal, the South China Sea’s most contested area, came after a confrontation on Monday at the disputed Second Thomas Shoal, 650km (400 miles) away from where Manila said a Filipino sailor was injured after being hit with a baton by Chinese coastguard personnel.Beijing had accused the Philippine Navy of ignoring warnings and of trying to ram a Chinese patrol boat. Both countries have summoned each other’s ambassadors in protest.But China has long occupied the Scarborough Shoal, deploying an armada of coastguard ships throughout the South China Sea.Beijing also claims sovereignty over nearly all of the waterway, including parts of the Exclusive Economic Zone of Brunei, Indonesia, Malaysia, the Philippines and Vietnam.The tensions also come as Southeast Asian foreign ministers finished regional meetings in Manila that were attended by Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi and United States Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who called Beijing’s actions in the sea “disturbing”, reaffirming support for the Philippines, with which the US has a defence treaty.
contentParagraphs.zh: 菲律宾海岸警卫队表示，有 12 艘中国海警船只和三艘中国海上民兵船只参与其中。马尼拉海岸警卫队指责中国海警连续第二天向争议浅滩附近的政府船只发射高压水枪后，中国和菲律宾之间的紧张局势仍在持续。菲律宾海岸警卫队周五表示，一艘中国船只在菲律宾专属经济区黄岩岛靠近一艘菲律宾渔船约七米（23 英尺）范围内，造成“严重的碰撞风险”。推荐故事 列表 3 项 列表 1（共 3 个）、中国、“黑暗工厂”和美国汽车工业的未来 列表 2（共 3 个） 随着霍尔木兹、曼德海峡关闭，航运保险费率如何上涨 列表 3（共 3 个） 特朗普对数十个国家征收新的两位数关税列表结束“PCG 强烈谴责中国海警的危险和不专业行为，”发言人杰伊塔里埃拉在一份声明中说。塔里埃拉补充说，该浅滩是菲律宾人的传统渔场，“菲律宾人的权利不能受到任何外国势力的合法阻碍”。海警补充说，12艘中国海警船和3艘中国海上民兵船只参与了此次事件，高压水枪击中了两艘菲律宾船只，另外两艘船只不得不做出危险动作，冒着相撞的风险。这些船只当时正在向菲律宾渔民运送燃油补贴，并进行例行巡逻。黄岩岛也被当地人称为巴霍德马辛洛克，位于菲律宾专属经济区内。周五早些时候，中国海警表示，已对几艘据称在黄岩岛周边海域非法作业的菲律宾船只实施“管制措施”。在一份更详细的声明中，中国海警指责菲律宾组织多艘政府和海警船只以及大量渔船在该海域“非法聚集”。在菲律宾官方船只无视海警的这些措施并“执意闯入”后，菲律宾官方船只采取了警告、拦截和部署高压水枪的措施。周四也发生了类似事件。周五，中国外交部表示海警的行为“合理合法，无可非议。”外交部发言人林健在例行吹风会上表示，“菲方应立即停止侵权、挑衅和炒作”。黄岩岛是南海争议最激烈的地区，此前周一在有争议的仁爱礁发生了一场对峙。仁爱礁距马尼拉650公里（400英里），马尼拉称一名菲律宾水手被中国海岸警卫队人员用警棍击中受伤。北京指责菲律宾海军无视警告并试图撞击一艘中国巡逻艇。两国均已召见对方大使以示抗议。但中国长期以来一直占领着黄岩岛，并在整个南海部署了一支由海岸警卫队组成的舰队。北京还声称对几乎整个水道拥有主权，其中包括文莱、印度尼西亚、马来西亚、菲律宾和越南部分专属经济区。紧张局势还发生在东南亚各国外长在马尼拉结束地区会议之际，中国外交部长王毅和美国国务卿马可·卢比奥出席了这些会议。海上行动“令人不安”，重申了对菲律宾的支持，美国与菲律宾签订了防御条约。

articles.title: ‘Customers prefer AI chatbots,’ says British Gas owner as 1,300 call centre jobs axed
articles.summary:
CEO Chris O’Shea defends Centrica’s plans as it reports rise in retail profits following focus on bigger margins
The owner of British Gas has claimed that most households would rather speak with an AI chatbot than deal with the company’s staff as it prepares to cut 1,300 jobs fro
articles.titleZh: 英国天然气公司老板在裁员 1,300 个呼叫中心工作后表示，“客户更喜欢人工智能聊天机器人”
articles.summaryZh:
首席执行官 Chris O’Shea 为 Centrica 的计划辩护，该公司报告称，由于专注于提高利润率，零售利润有所增长
英国天然气公司 (British Gas) 的老板声称，在准备裁员 1,300 人之际，大多数家庭宁愿与人工智能聊天机器人交谈，也不愿与该公司员工打交道。

contentParagraphs.en: The owner of British Gas has claimed that most households would rather speak with an AI chatbot than deal with the company’s staff as it prepares to cut 1,300 jobs from its call centres.Centrica, the supplier’s FTSE 100 owner, plans to cut 800 jobs as the company carries out a “targeted deployment of AI tools”, on top of the 500 cuts it confirmed last month.The plan to reduce the supplier’s customer service teams in Glasgow, Edinburgh, Cardiff, Leicester, Stockport and Leeds by 14% is expected to take place over two years, meaning some roles will be left unfilled after resignations, while the balance will come from redundancies.Trade unions have previously said the company’s investment in artificial intelligence to help simplify its business would lead to “hundreds of human jobs” being given to chatbots.But Chris O’Shea, the company’s chief executive, insisted that the changes were being made because customers preferred to take their queries to digital channels rather than people.“AI isn’t driving these particular job reductions; that’s mainly due to changing customer behaviour,” he said. “What we’re seeing just now is over 90% of our customers actually use digital channels in the first instance, and we’ve seen a 20% reduction in customer calls, so this simply reflects the changing customer behaviour.“We’ve got to reflect the changes in customer behaviour. We expect to grow more jobs around our digital interface, and maybe having fewer people on the phones.”O’Shea defence of Centrica’s plans came as it reported a rise in retail profits over the first half of the year despite falling customer numbers.British Gas has gradually lost customers to rival suppliers in recent years. It is expected to pay out up to £112m in compensation to thousands of British Gas customers who had prepayment meters force-fitted in their homes at the height of the Russian gas crisis in the biggest energy supplier settlement on record.skip past newsletter promotionafter newsletter promotionProfits from Centrica’s retail division, which includes revenues from British Gas as well as its boiler care and smart energy products, rose to £346m in the first six months of the year, from £338m in the same period last year.Its profits climbed even as the number of British Gas domestic customers fell to 7.45 million, from 7.5 million at the end of last year, because the supplier has focused on making bigger profit margins from its fixed-price tariffs rather than “chase loss-making business”, O’Shea said.
contentParagraphs.zh: 英国天然气公司的所有者声称，大多数家庭宁愿与人工智能聊天机器人交谈，也不愿与该公司的员工打交道，因为该公司准备从其呼叫中心裁员 1,300 人。该供应商的富时 100 指数所有者 Centrica 在上个月确认裁员 500 人的基础上，还计划裁员 800 人，因为该公司将进行“有针对性的人工智能工具部署”。计划削减该供应商在格拉斯哥的客户服务团队，爱丁堡、卡迪夫、莱斯特、斯托克波特和利兹预计将在两年内减少 14%，这意味着一些职位将在辞职后空缺，而其余职位将来自裁员。工会此前表示，该公司为帮助简化业务而对人工智能的投资将导致“数百个人类工作岗位”被交给聊天机器人。但该公司首席执行官 Chris O’Shea 坚称，做出这些改变是因为客户更愿意通过数字渠道而不是人来询问。“人工智能并没有推动这些特定的工作岗位减少；这主要是由于客户行为的改变，”他说。 “我们现在看到的是，超过 90% 的客户实际上首先使用了数字渠道，而且客户来电数量减少了 20%，因此这只是反映了客户行为的变化。“我们必须反映客户行为的变化。 “我们希望围绕数字界面创造更多就业机会，也许会减少打电话的人数。”O'Shea 为 Centrica 的计划辩护，因为该公司报告称，尽管客户数量下降，但今年上半年零售利润却有所上升。近年来，英国天然气公司的客户逐渐被竞争对手供应商夺走。预计将向数千名英国天然气公司客户支付高达 1.12 亿英镑的赔偿，这些客户在俄罗斯天然气危机最严重的时候，在家里安装了预付费电表，这是一项最大的能源供应商和解协议。 Centrica 零售部门的利润（包括来自英国天然气公司及其锅炉维护和智能能源产品的收入），从去年同期的 3.38 亿英镑增至今年前 6 个月的 3.46 亿英镑。尽管英国天然气公司的国内客户数量从去年年底的 750 万下降至 745 万，但该部门的利润仍在攀升，因为该供应商专注于获取更大的利润奥谢表示，他们的目标是从固定价格关税中获取利润，而不是“追逐亏损业务”。

articles.title: We split bills equally even when one of us earned a lot more
articles.summary: Hannah and Max continued to pool finances after Max was made redundant but took "drastic measures" to cut spending.
articles.titleZh: 即使我们中的一个人赚得多，我们也会平均分摊账单
articles.summaryZh: 麦克斯被解雇后，汉娜和麦克斯继续筹集资金，但采取了“严厉措施”来削减开支。
